text.title: TDD Workflow — Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when tdd-workflow is mis-applied, each with symptom, root cause, and concrete fix.

antipattern.description: Implementation written, then tests added that validate what was written.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Tests rationalise the existing code; they prove nothing about the spec. Fix: Discard the tests; write fresh RED tests against the spec.
Root cause: Cannot tell which assert the next implementation fixed. Fix: Split into five RED-GREEN cycles.
Root cause: Code stays in 'minimal' shape forever; structural debt compounds. Fix: Always run the refactor step; commit separately.
Root cause: Cannot tell whether the refactor broke things or it was already broken. Fix: Get to GREEN first; then refactor.
